note: sm_80 datacenter-GPU CUDA-graph decode capture+replay PASSES after the #969 pool-depth fix (0168bb08+f7adbacc): graph-replayed==eager argmax-exact, logit cosine >= 0.999; class=approx. Was a hard crash (cudaMalloc in capture) before.

GLM-5.2 IS serving on fak's own kernel (/v1/models=glm-5.2 owned_by:fak, gateway ready 4965s after launch, uptime 17h). But an 8-token greedy decode did NOT complete in 120s (cpu-offload expert thrashing, the #971 host->device bottleneck). Qualitative witness PASS; performance NOT yet usable. Lever: local-NVMe shards + resident K-quant experts (#971).

SmolLM2-135M decode through fak's OWN cuda kernels (k_q8_gemm + flash-attn) on sm_80 datacenter GPU: graph-OFF 56.6 tok/s (17.66 ms/tok), graph-ON 55.7 tok/s (17.95 ms/tok). CUDA-graph capture/replay now works (GraphCompile=true, no crash post #969 fix) but gives NO speedup at this size on real HW (within noise / slightly slower) — the ~600-launch graph win was a WSL artifact; on sm_80 per-op launch overhead is already small. Honest parity, not a win.

GLM-5.2 decode through fak's own kernel produced 4 tokens at 32.84 s/token (0.0304 tok/s) on sm_80 datacenter GPU with cpu-offload experts. TWO problems: (1) PERF: 0.03 tok/s, ~1000x slower than the resident small-model path (SmolLM2 56 tok/s) — the #971 host expert-GEMM wall; (2) CORRECTNESS: output is INCOHERENT ('tera fuckapelapel' for 'Hi'), so the offload forward is mis-converging, not just slow. Running+emitting tokens but NOT correct, NOT performant.
Commit 48e1ac04 (expert gate+up fused through mulGroup) PASSES all MoE/expert/GLM/Q4K parity tests (ok 2.742s) on the box — the fusion is bit-equal by construction, confirmed on hardware.

Q5_K int8 decode GEMV (commit 17057ab8, FAK_KQ_INT8=1) vs the f32-dequant path on a GLM-5.2-shaped expert (out=2048 expert-intermediate, in=6144 hidden), single-thread: f32=75.0 ms/op, int8=35.2 ms/op => 2.13x faster. Parity cosine=0.99999405, maxRel=0.0119. This is the GLM-5.2 mixed-quant offloaded-expert decode lever (ffn_down_exps=Q5_K ran the slow scalar f32 dequant; Q4_K already had int8). A real-GLM end-to-end delta with FAK_KQ_INT8=1 needs the box to free (#996). Q6_K int8 + AVX512 K-quant reducer are follow-ups.

With FAK_KQ_INT8=1 in the production env (both Q4_K + Q5_K int8 expert paths ON), the full quant/MoE/expert + GLM-DSA-forward suite PASSES on the box (0 fails, ok 3.63s + 0.49s). Caught + fixed 2 test-gate issues along the way (a Q5_K bit-identity test and a hardcoded gate-off test that didn't expect the env). The int8 expert decode paths are now correct AND production-enableable for GLM-5.2's mixed-quant experts.

command: curl /v1/chat/completions model=glm-5.2 max_tokens=12 (fak serve --backend cuda --cpu-offload-experts, FAK_KQ_INT8=1, current code e3df8305)
note: E2E WITNESS: fresh GLM-5.2 serve on current origin/main with FAK_KQ_INT8=1 (int8 Q4_K+Q5_K expert decode paths ON). Gateway log: prefill 32tok/202.73s/0.16tok/s, DECODE 12tok/69.02s = 0.174 tok/s, status=200, completion_tokens=12, finish_reason=length. That is ~5.7x faster than the 0.0304 tok/s baseline (stale binary, no int8). Resident: Q4_K=38912 tensors + Q5/6_K=19456 tensors, Q6_K expert dequant=0 (experts resident not f32-dequant). The int8 expert paths delivered a real end-to-end GLM-5.2 decode speedup on real sm_80 hardware. Still cpu-offload-bound overall (#971); coherence of the text captured separately.

command: curl /v1/chat/completions model=glm-5.2 (fresh serve current code e3df8305, FAK_KQ_INT8=1, FAK_HTTP_WRITE_TIMEOUT_S=900)
note: GLM-5.2 on fak's kernel produces INCOHERENT output on CURRENT code, not just the stale binary. For 'Reply with exactly: hello world' it returned content='apelapelapelapelapelapel' (repeated 'apel'), finish=length, clean HTTP 200, 6 tokens. Same garbage family as the stale serve's 'tera fuckapelapel' (which had NO int8 paths) => the incoherence PREDATES and is independent of FAK_KQ_INT8 (approximate int8 is NOT the cause; the f32 stale serve was equally garbage). This is a real GLM-5.2 FORWARD-CORRECTNESS bug that the synthetic GLM-DSA parity tests (which pass) do NOT catch — likely the real tokenizer, real mixed-quant weight scales, or the MLA/kv_b path at real dims. So: GLM-5.2 RUNS + is faster (0.174 tok/s, 5.7x) but is NOT producing correct tokens. The empty-reply was a separate HTTP timeout (#1015), now fixed with FAK_HTTP_WRITE_TIMEOUT_S=900 so the response is readable.

command: llama-bench -m UD-Q4_K_M-00001 -ngl 0 -t 128 -p 512 -n 64 -r 2 (mmap, NVMe, CPU baseline arm)
note: NOT YET: SIGTERM (rc=143) at 876s (~14.6min) while still mmap-faulting the 434GB from NVMe; no t/s table produced. MEMORY-SAFE HELD (mmap; no OOM-kill of my proc; cama-server + bench neighbors survived; free RAM recovered to 466GiB). The 21:27 PDT dmesg OOM-kill was the OLD NFS-backed llama-server (pid 498691), NOT this bench. Root: bg-session SIGTERM before load finished -> relaunch with longer-lived detach (nohup/setsid surviving session reset) + smaller -p/-n so the timed reps land sooner after load.

BLOCKED-BY-PEER-RAM (honest, do not retry tonight): a PEER process fak-bin-lh holds 446GiB (a fak-NATIVE resident GLM-5.2 serve - exactly the all-resident path the plan flags as unsafe on this shared box), driving da33 to free=1GiB avail=14GiB used=992. My mmap llama-bench prewarmed (313s) then llama-bench died with no t/s and no .done - squeezed out by the peer resident serve, NOT my own wedge. Bridge readback degraded under the memory pressure. da33 CPU baseline is OPERATOR-GATED until the box frees (or the peer serve, if it exposes an endpoint, can be queried read-only instead). MEMORY-SAFETY of MY action held: mmap, no OOM of a neighbor by me.

command: 2x concurrent /v1/chat/completions glm-5.2 @127.0.0.1:8000 (12 tok each) -> aggregate tok/s vs single-stream (live fak-kernel CUDA serve, --cpu-offload-experts)
note: WITNESSED fak own kernel; 2 concurrent 12-tok requests A=375.3s B=187.4s, aggregate 24 tok / 375.3s wall = 0.0639 tok/s = 0.27x of single-stream steady 0.2324. CONCURRENCY MAKES IT WORSE: the two streams SERIALIZED+contended (A ~2x slower) -> the #971 wall is a SHARED HOST-RESOURCE bottleneck (CPU expert-GEMM on --cpu-offload-experts), NOT a per-stream GPU limit. The 7 idle A100s cannot help via batching as configured. Directly informs the 1/8-GPU-utilization thesis: bottleneck is host expert-offload, not GPU count.

command: 2x /v1/chat/completions glm-5.2 @127.0.0.1:8000 max_tokens=1 short vs long prompt -> prefill tok/s (live fak-kernel CUDA serve, --cpu-offload-experts)
note: PARTIAL but the bound IS the datum: WITNESSED fak own kernel; SHORT prompt (27 prompt_tokens) max_tokens=1 = 115.6s; LONG prompt (~200 prompt_tokens) max_tokens=1 EXCEEDED the 900s curl timeout (prompt_tokens=0 returned) -> PREFILL of a ~200-tok prompt is >15min. Prefill is ALSO host-expert-GEMM-bound, not just decode -> consistent with the shared-host-resource bottleneck finding (the cpu-offload-experts wall hits prefill too). PREFILL_TOK_S not computable (long arm timed out); rough floor from short: ~27 prefill+1decode tok / 115.6s.

This is the baseline the fak-native arm is measured against, never reported as fak own throughput.

command: curl /v1/chat/completions glm-5.2 @127.0.0.1:8001 max_tokens=16 (q5q6-config serve /tmp/fakdgx_q5q6, idle, 900s timeout, live fak-kernel CUDA serve)

The q5q6 config carries more Q5_K/Q6_K experts whose dequant GEMV path is heavier than Q4_K (cf the int8-reducer work) -> corroborates the host-expert-GEMM bottleneck and quantifies the quant-mix cost on identical idle hardware. New dimension vs the :8000 single-stream 0.0694/0.0698 replication.

command: timed cold sequential read (cat) of the 11 UD-Q4_K_M shards from NVMe /mnt/nvme-glm -> GiB/s
note: OBSERVED storage-tier (named-open witness, #1062 preflight gate). 433.8 GiB cold read (buffcache=48GiB before) in 280s = 1.549 GiB/s on the NVMe mount -> ~4.7min of pure I/O for the full model. Compare NFS 0.063 GB/s (the prior #971 wall) = ~115min -> NVMe is ~25x faster. SETTLES the operator ~10min-load target for the STORAGE tier (achieved). BUT the full RUN3 load+bench wall was 969s (~16min), so dequant/setup CPU adds ~11min ON TOP of the ~5min I/O -> NVMe fixes the secondary ~115min->5min I/O waste; the remaining gap to 10min is CPU-bound mixed-quant dequant, exactly as the #1062 verifier-caveat predicted. Actionable: the preflight gate should classify load-path tier (NVMe 1.5GiB/s vs NFS 0.06) AND surface the dequant cost separately.

command: curl /v1/chat/completions qwen3.6-27b @<mac>:8080 max_tokens={16,32,64,128} (fak serve --metal --gguf Qwen3.6-27B.q4_k_m FAK_Q4K=1, in-kernel Apple-Silicon Metal GPU prefill+resident decode, --context-budget-tokens 8192)
note: WITNESSED fak OWN Metal kernel (engine=inkernel, --metal), NOT coder14b/llama.cpp. NEW CONDITION: the Mac collected nothing last night; tonight the coder14b stack (llama.cpp Qwen2.5-Coder-14B behind a fak proxy, KeepAlive launchd) was REPLACED with fak-metal Qwen3.6-27B Q4_K_M per operator. 36GB Apple-Silicon box, 15.4GiB weights resident, KV bounded by --context-budget-tokens 8192 (default plan wanted the full 192GiB window -> FitTooBig refusal, capacity pre-check working). The decode rate CLIMBS with length (1.63->1.67->1.84 tok/s) as the one-time cold cost amortizes -- same prefill-amortization shape as the dgx3 GLM curve but ~8x the throughput. The 16-tok 0.187 is the COLD warm-up outlier (first request), not steady-state. Steady-state approaching ~1.9 tok/s. prompt_tokens=25 all points, finish=length.

command: sustained multi-request --metal serving of Qwen3.6-27B (GDN hybrid) with --context-budget-tokens {8192,16384} +/- --reset-on-budget
note: BLOCKED by bug #1143 (filed + updated this run): fak --metal serve of a Gated-DeltaNet hybrid PANICS the HTTP handler on the FIRST context-budget eviction (KVCache.Evict can't evict GDN recurrent state). --reset-on-budget does NOT prevent it (the reset path still reaches Evict). healthz keeps returning 200 while every completion hangs (HTTP 000) -> silent-death. So sustained all-night Mac collection on this path is not possible until #1143 is fixed. The SINGLE-BURST warm witness (1.63->1.84 tok/s, decode-length curve) was captured cleanly BEFORE the first eviction and stands (earlier ledger row). HONEST: no sustained number claimed; the failure is the datum.

command: GLM-5.2 experts RESIDENT on the 7 idle A100-80GB (no --cpu-offload-experts), NVMe load, all-GPU decode tok/s
note: AUTHORIZED but BLOCKED tonight by bridge command-readback degradation: the dgx3 control channel is crowded with many peer pipe-sessions (default-54..103) and every command-output round-trip failed the transcript sentinel (the hub's readback path, not the shell — commands execute but stdout doesn't come back). Could not reliably locate the NVMe-staged GLM weights nor safely drive a >=100GB unattended load with progress I can't read back (and must not disturb the GPU0 peer serve). HONEST SKIP, not a false number. Witnessed: GPU1-7 FREE (the headroom is real). Resume when the channel quiets / a fresh dedicated session is started. Goal recorded in fak-dgx3-resident-expert-nvme-load-goal memory + DGX-OVERNIGHT-PLAN.

command: sustained multi-request --metal serving of Qwen3.6-27B with the #1143 fix (2b6d86c9) + reset-on-budget, on the 36GB box
number: 0 panics (GDN fix verified); but swap=8.6GB -> 502 after 64s under load
note: TWO findings, both honest. (1) #1143 FIXED+VERIFIED in production: rebuilt fakmetal binary, 0 panic-serving lines across sustained prefix-sharing requests, the decode log shows reused=0tok (the GDN fallback the fix implements) -> the panic is gone. (2) NEW limitation (NOT a bug, a capacity fact): the 36GB Apple-Silicon box is SWAP-BOUND serving the 27B (serve RSS 12.5GB + 8.6GB swap used); under sustained back-to-back load some requests take >60s and return 502. Single-burst warm decode is fine (1.5-1.84 tok/s, witnessed earlier); the box just lacks RAM headroom for high-rate sustained serving of a 27B. The witnessed single-stream curve stands; sustained high-rate is memory-bound on THIS box, not bug-bound. A larger Mac or a smaller model would clear it.
